name: Draupadi
description: Draupadi, also known as Krishnaa, Panchali, and Yagnyaseni, is a central female character in the Hindu epic Mahabharata. Born from a sacrificial fire, she is renowned for her beauty, intelligence, and unwavering devotion to dharma. Draupadi becomes the common wife of the five Pandava brothers, a union born out of a misunderstanding and a promise made by Kunti. Her most famous episode is the disrobing in the Kaurava court, where her plea to Krishna saves her honor. This act and the subsequent events lead to the Kurukshetra War. Draupadi represents strength, resilience, and the fight for justice against injustice. Her trials and tribulations underscore themes of dharma, karma, and the consequences of adharma. She is revered as a symbol of female power and a reminder of the importance of upholding righteousness even in the face of adversity. Her life is a complex tapestry of joy, sorrow, and unwavering faith. Her story is a potent reminder of the consequences of pride and the ultimate triumph of good over evil. Draupadi's five husbands represent different aspects of dharma, and her relationship with each is unique and significant.
subtitle: The fiery queen of the Pandavas.
achievements: Symbol of female strength and resilience

keyMyths.title: Birth from the Fire Altar
keyMyths.description: Draupadi was born from a sacrificial fire performed by King Drupada, along with her twin brother Dhrishtadyumna. This unusual birth marked her destiny and connected her to the lineage of warriors destined to fight for dharma.

keyMyths.title: The Disrobing of Draupadi
keyMyths.description: During a game of dice, Yudhishthira lost Draupadi as a wager, and she was dragged into the court and disrobed by Dushasana. Her prayers to Krishna resulted in an endless supply of fabric, saving her honor and enraging the Kauravas.

keyMyths.title: Vengeance and the Kurukshetra War
keyMyths.description: The humiliation Draupadi suffered fueled her desire for revenge against the Kauravas. Her vow to not tie her hair until it was washed with the blood of Dushasana contributed to the events leading up to the Kurukshetra War.

sources.author: Vyasa
sources.description: The primary source for Draupadi's story and the epic tale of the Pandavas and Kauravas.
sources.text: The primary source for Draupadi's story and the epic tale of the Pandavas and Kauravas.

sources.author: Irawati Karve
